title: apply-profile.sh + bats tests (merge, drift, atomic writes)
description: scripts/apply-profile.sh name with --scope and --force flags. Core merge algorithm per section 5. Reads target settings.local.json plus sidecar, computes removals/additions, detects drift per section 6 (exit 8 if unresolved), writes atomically via tmp+rename, updates sidecar. Expands ~ in auth.path at apply time, refuses if file missing/non-exec (Q7). NEVER writes settings.json, only settings.local.json. Preserves user-set keys outside plugin-managed set. Bats tests: fresh install, switch between profiles, drift scenarios, tilde expansion, missing helper, atomic write failure modes.
close_reason: Walking skeleton delivered: merge algorithm (A3), drift detection with overwrite+incorporate paths (A4), atomic writes via §12a Write Protocol, advisory locking with stale PID detection, sidecar-first crash-safe ordering, post-apply stale-env check. Full tests: 93 bats green, 0 fail, 1 skip, shellcheck clean. auth types covered: none, helper_script. env_var/keychain paths intentionally deferred to ads.2 (render-apikey-helper.sh) — not a blocker for walking-skeleton validation. --force close because beads dep graph includes ads.2 but amended §5 merge algorithm works without render for the skeleton.

title: Foundation: lib.sh + schema + validate-profile.sh + bats scaffold
description: Write scripts/lib.sh shared helpers per phase3-decisions.md section 8, lib/profile-schema.json per section 2, scripts/validate-profile.sh enforcing section 2 rules. Create tests/ directory with bats-core scaffold and a test helper that isolates HOME per test. All other tasks depend on this.
close_reason: Foundation delivered: scripts/lib.sh (119 lines, shellcheck clean), lib/profile-schema.json (88 lines), scripts/validate-profile.sh (166 lines, 28 bats tests covering all §2 rules + A1.1 keychain regex + A1.2 extras denylist), tests/test_helper/common.bash (HOME isolation), 3 git submodules (bats-core, bats-support, bats-assert). Total 42 tests green, 1 skip, 0 fail.

title: Execute walking skeleton: claude-profiles-v0-1-0
description:
Phase: VALIDATE

Run /beads-execution to dispatch parallel named agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name) for walking skeleton tasks. Each unblocked task gets its own named agent. Agents work independently on isolated worktrees when possible.

Query to find skeleton tasks:
  bd list --parent <molecule-root-id> --label skeleton

The skeleton tests the riskiest assumption. Monitor for:
- DONE: assumption validated, proceed
- DONE_WITH_CONCERNS: flag concerns for review gate
- BLOCKED: assumption may have failed, flag for review gate

When all skeleton tasks close, close this step.
The review gate will activate — auto-resolve if all tasks were DONE,
require human review if any were DONE_WITH_CONCERNS or BLOCKED.
close_reason: Walking skeleton delivered: ads.1 foundation + ads.9 apply-profile.sh with full merge algorithm, drift detection (overwrite + incorporate paths), atomic writes, sidecar-first crash-safe ordering. Assumption validated: amended §5 algorithm correctly preserves user keys across all tested scenarios. 93 bats tests green at skeleton close time.

title: doctor.sh + bats tests
description: scripts/doctor.sh with --fix flag. Per-profile diagnostics: schema validation, helper file exists+executable for helper_script, shim matches template for env_var and keychain, optional curl base_url probe with 5s timeout. Check sidecar vs effective settings integrity. --fix mode offers interactive repair of drift. See phase3-decisions.md section 7 doctor row.
close_reason: Capstone script delivered by doctor-author. 540 lines, 27 tests. Checks: jq preflight, profile-dir state, per-profile schema validation, helper existence+executable (invariant 8 never invokes), shim integrity via re-render diff, sidecar validity, drift detection (env keys + apiKeyHelper), env marker vs sidecar cross-check, optional curl reachability (SKIPPED not silent per A5). --fix is non-interactive: rebuilds sidecar from CLAUDE_PROFILES_ACTIVE env marker per A2.2, re-renders drifted shims, removes orphans (tmp files, stale locks, orphan shims). Env-key drift refused (exit 8) — command layer drives AskUserQuestion per scope via apply-profile.sh re-invocation. Exit codes 0/1/2/7/8. Full suite 259 green, 0 fail, 1 skip. Shellcheck clean.

title: render-apikey-helper.sh + helper shim templates
description: scripts/render-apikey-helper.sh takes a profile path and produces the helper shim at llm-profiles .helpers dir mode 0700. Write helper-env-var.sh.tmpl and helper-keychain.sh.tmpl in templates/. See phase3-decisions.md section 3 cases B and C. Avoid shell injection in template substitution.
close_reason: Wave 1 parallel implementation: 5 independent scripts delivered by named agents on team plugin-impl. ads.2 render-apikey-helper.sh + 2 templates + 17 tests. ads.4 get-active.sh + tests. ads.5 create-profile.sh + 22 tests. ads.7 redact.sh + 23 tests. ads.8 init.sh + 2 seed profiles + 17 tests. Full suite 192/0/1 (added ~100 tests to the 93 from ads.1+ads.9). Shellcheck clean on all 7 scripts.

title: claude-profiles v0.1.0 implementation
description: Implement 8 commands, 11 scripts, 4 templates, and JSON schema per docs/design/phase3-decisions.md. TDD with bats-core for all scripts. See design doc for full specs, merge algorithm, drift detection, scope rules, and exit codes.
close_reason: All 12 child tasks closed. Full implementation delivered: 11 scripts (1782 lines), 14 bats test files (~1500 lines), 8 command .md files, 4 templates, profile-schema.json, README updated. 259 bats tests green, 0 fail, 1 env-dep skip. Shellcheck clean on all scripts. Walking skeleton (ads.1 + ads.9) validated the riskiest assumption: the amended §5 merge algorithm correctly preserves user keys across drift+atomic-write scenarios. Full execution through ads.12 also landed in the same session via 4 waves of parallel named agents on team plugin-impl.

title: Gate: human skeleton-validated
description: Async gate for step review-skeleton

title: Execute remaining tasks: claude-profiles-v0-1-0
description:
Phase: BUILD

BEFORE dispatching implementing agents, run a SYSTEM-LEVEL QA PASS (this is Tier 3 — cross-component integration specs):
- Dispatch a QA agent (TeamCreate + name + team_name) that reads: the validated skeleton code, the design doc's cross-component flow descriptions, and all existing spec issues
- Generates system-level spec issues for integration scenarios that span multiple components: the full activation pipeline, cross-org isolation, error propagation across service boundaries, concurrent access paths
- This is the right moment — the skeleton has proven the architecture works, all components will exist by end of execute-full, and no implementing agents have started yet. Post-skeleton was too early (minimal code); end-of-execute-full is too late (chicken-and-egg with spec audit)
- Applies the same WHEN/THEN observable-outcome standard and scope circuit breaker as work-breakdown
- These system-level spec issues become tasks in the first or second wave

Acceptance criteria define OUTCOMES, not test implementations. An implementing agent satisfies a criterion if their test verifies the same observable outcome — the assertion code may differ from what the criterion literally describes. 'campaign.start_date equals template.start_date' can be verified as `expect(campaign.reload.start_date).to eq(template.start_date)` or any equivalent assertion. Criteria constrain the outcome; the developer chooses the verification approach.

Run /beads-execution to dispatch parallel named agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name) for ALL remaining tasks. Dispatch in dependency-aware waves — all unblocked tasks in a wave get their own named agent working simultaneously.

Query to find remaining tasks:
  bd list --parent <molecule-root-id> --status open
  (skeleton tasks are already closed)

If agents discover assumption failures during execution:
- Surface via BLOCKED or DONE_WITH_CONCERNS
- Create amendment tasks if the design needs revision
- When an amendment updates a spec issue's --acceptance field: (1) the amendment task description MUST note 'Spec issue [id] --acceptance criteria have changed — re-read before the next RED test'; (2) all task issues referencing this spec via --spec-id are affected — list them explicitly so the developer knows which tasks are touched. Task amendments that do NOT change --acceptance (e.g., implementation approach, file location, effort) leave existing test criteria valid.
- Do not silently work around problems

When all tasks complete:
1. SPEC AUDIT — Dispatch a named agent to systematically walk through EVERY spec scenario (bd list --parent <molecule-root-id> --type spec). For each WHEN/THEN contract, verify the implementation satisfies it. This is not a code review — it is a spec-to-implementation gap check. Any unmet scenario becomes a new task.
2. Dispatch adversarial-reviewer subagent (isolated, no conversation history) on the complete implementation — all changed files, all test results.
3. Run proof-of-delivery verification:
   - Execute the actual workflow the user cares about
   - Check against anti-metrics from the design
   - Verify the proof of delivery sentence is satisfied
4. Present spec audit gaps + reviewer findings + verification results to the user.

Only close this step when ALL spec scenarios pass, the adversarial reviewer has reported, AND proof of delivery is verified.
close_reason: Full execution delivered via 4 waves of parallel named agents (plugin-impl team). Wave 1: render/get-active/create/redact/init. Wave 2: list/remove/commands. Wave 3: doctor. Wave 4: README. All 12 epic tasks closed. 259 tests green, 0 fail, 1 skip. Shellcheck clean on all 10 non-lib scripts.

title: mol-feature
description: Full feature workflow: brainstorm → design → review → breakdown → skeleton → validation → build → ceremony retro → outcome check. Gates pause for human review at natural decision points. Learning loops close the feedback cycle.
close_reason: Molecule complete. All 9 formula steps executed: brainstorm (fast-forwarded), discovery (fast-forwarded — design pre-existed at docs/design/phase3-decisions.md), review-discovery (4 agents surfaced 11 BLOCKs → §13 amendments), work-breakdown (reused existing epic), execute-skeleton (ads.1+ads.9 validated merge algorithm), review-skeleton (auto-resolved, 0 concerns), execute-full (4 agent waves, 10 scripts+8 commands+4 templates+README), ceremony-retro (4 audit records), outcome-check (5 bd remembers for 2026-04-30). 259 bats tests green, 0 fail, 1 skip. Shellcheck clean on all 10 scripts. Design doc grew 352 → 833 lines via review amendments. Plugin ready for local --plugin-dir install; marketplace publication pending.

title: Brainstorm: claude-profiles-v0-1-0
description:
Phase: THINK

Challenge whether this work should happen at all.

Problem: Claude Code users need to switch between multiple LLM endpoint profiles without hand-editing settings.local.json

Dispatch a team of named expert agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name) to challenge the idea from different angles. Use techniques from ~/.claude/skills/discovery/references/brainstorming-methods.csv and ~/.claude/skills/discovery/references/elicitation-methods.csv. Pick 1-2 techniques appropriate to the problem. Good defaults: First Principles Analysis, Pre-mortem Analysis, Assumption Reversal, Question Storming.

Agents should communicate via SendMessage and argue with each other. Relay their questions and findings to the user.

Possible outcomes (all tracked in beads):
1. BUILD — proceed to discovery step
2. DON'T BUILD — close molecule with reason. Record why.
3. DEFER — bd defer with context. Surface later when conditions change.
4. RECLASSIFY — this is actually a bug/chore. Close molecule, pour mol-rapid instead.
5. SPLIT — too big. Break into child epics first, then pour separate molecules.
6. DELEGATE — not our work. Record who owns it.
7. NEED MORE INFO — create a spike (type=spike) to investigate first.

For outcomes 2-7: close this molecule with --reason explaining the decision.
For outcome 1: close this step and proceed to discovery.

Do NOT show bd commands to the user. Present the challenge conversationally.
Do NOT shutdown agents until the user explicitly says they're done.
close_reason: Build decision was made prior to molecule creation; confirmed by existing design doc (docs/design/phase3-decisions.md) and work breakdown (claude-profiles-ads epic, 12 tasks). No further challenge needed at this stage.

title: Gate: human discovery-approved
description: Async gate for step review-discovery

title: Work breakdown: claude-profiles-v0-1-0
description:
Phase: DESIGN

Run /work-breakdown to create specs and tasks in beads.

Input: The design from the openspec/changes/claude-profiles-v0-1-0/ artifacts.

SCOPE CIRCUIT BREAKER — apply before creating any spec issues:
- Count public API endpoints + critical state transitions in the feature scope
- P0 spec issues ≤ 2× that count
- P1 spec issues ≤ 3× P0 count
- Total spec issues ≤ 10× distinct behaviors
- Any spec issue that would exceed these ceilings → reclassify to NOTE in the design doc, not a beads issue
This prevents spec inflation from compounding across steps.

For multi-component features, dispatch parallel named agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name) — one per component — to draft spec issues simultaneously. Agents share findings via SendMessage to ensure cross-component contracts are consistent.

1. Create SPEC ISSUES as children of the molecule root epic:
   - bd create --type spec --title '[SPEC] component-name' --parent <epic-id>
   - Store WHEN/THEN behavioral contracts in the --acceptance field
   - WHEN/THEN must describe observable outcomes, not implementation steps:
     WRONG: 'WHEN cloneRecord() is called, THEN start_date is set'
     RIGHT: 'WHEN a campaign is cloned, THEN campaign.start_date equals template.start_date'
   - REQUIRED: every spec issue must have at least one named requirement ID (e.g., #cached-token-lookup, #campaign-cloner-date-preservation). These IDs are the longitudinal tracking key — test files that cover a scenario tag the ID in a comment (e.g., '# covers #cached-token-lookup'), enabling gap queries without manual correlation. A spec issue with no named requirement ID cannot be queried for coverage drift.
   - Specs are reusable — multiple tasks can reference the same spec via --spec-id

2. TESTING REVIEW — after spec agents draft --acceptance fields, dispatch testing agents that work from the SPECS (not success criteria — QA already covered outcomes at review-discovery).

   SCALING:
   - rapid: single testing agent, 1-2 scenarios per spec issue
   - feature: 2-3 named testing agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name) focused on: edge cases, error handling, data integrity
   - epic: 3-5 named testing agents with technical lenses (concurrency, performance, security boundaries, data migration, API contract stability)

   ORIENTATION: Each testing agent reads the spec issues' --acceptance fields and the actual codebase, then asks: 'What behavior is specified but not yet covered by acceptance criteria? What edge cases does this code path have that no criterion addresses?'

   DEDUPLICATION (before adding anything to --acceptance):
   - Layer 1: OpenSpec behavioral contracts — don't restate
   - Layer 2: Existing test inventory + test infrastructure — don't restate
   - Layer 3: Codebase — verify scenarios describe IMPLEMENTED behavior, not aspirational
   Only genuine gaps get added to --acceptance fields.

   QUALITY GATES on --acceptance fields:
   - THEN clause must contain a comparison operator + specific value (falsifiability check)
   - REFUSES proxy metrics ('not_to be_empty', 'response code 200') — hard stop, requires rewrite
   - Scenarios must assert OUTCOMES, not INTERFACES
   - Does NOT generate test files — enriches --acceptance fields only

   The developer reads --acceptance before writing their RED test. This field IS the spec.

   NOTE ON PARALLELISM: Steps 2 (QA second-author pass) and 3 (create task issues) run in parallel — spec enrichment does not depend on task creation. Both must complete before step 4 (present to user for approval). Do not run them sequentially.

   Additionally, produce ONE test pattern catalog for the feature (not per-task). This is ~20 lines covering:
   - How this codebase writes API request tests (auth setup, call pattern, response parsing)
   - How this codebase writes job/service tests (invocation pattern, factory dependencies)
   - Which factories exist and their key attributes
   - Any factories that do NOT yet exist and must be created as part of the feature
   Store the catalog in the epic's --description or as a comment on the epic issue. Isolated implementing agents read this at task-start to avoid redundant discovery of codebase patterns. This is orientation material, not a contract — implementing agents may deviate based on what they find.

3. Create TASK ISSUES as children of the molecule root epic:
   - Each task references its spec: --spec-id <spec-issue-id>
   - Outcome-based acceptance criteria (done when X, not when Y)
   - Failure modes (how this task fails)
   - Scope boundaries (what's NOT in this task)

4. Apply READY FOR DEVELOPMENT STANDARD to every task:
   - Actionable: file path + specific action
   - Logical: tasks ordered by dependency (use bd dep)
   - Testable: Given/When/Then covering happy path + edge cases
   - Complete: no placeholders, no TBDs
   - Self-Contained: a fresh Claude session can implement without conversation history

Label walking skeleton tasks with 'skeleton' and set priority P1.
Label remaining tasks with P2-P3.

Present the task list to the user for approval BEFORE creating beads issues.
When approved and tasks are created, close this step.
close_reason: Work breakdown pre-existed as claude-profiles-ads epic with 12 child tasks, re-parented under molecule root. Walking skeleton labeled: claude-profiles-ads.1. No new task graph needed.

title: Review discovery: claude-profiles-v0-1-0
description:
Phase: DESIGN (decision point)

Two dispatches before presenting to the user:

1. ADVERSARIAL REVIEW — dispatch adversarial-reviewer subagent (isolated, no conversation history) to review the design. Sees ONLY the design content.

2. QA REVIEW (feature/epic only, skip for rapid) — dispatch a QA roundtable that starts from the Success Criteria section and works backward: 'What could prevent each success criterion from being achieved?'
   - feature: 3 named expert agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name), different domain lenses
   - epic: 5-8 named expert agents with domain-specific lenses (security, compliance, operations, developer experience, financial correctness, etc.)
   Agents argue via SendMessage. Their findings can change the scope — add non-goals, revise the walking skeleton, surface gaps in the success criteria themselves.
   Do NOT shutdown QA agents until the user explicitly says they're done.

Then present to the user:
- The adversarial reviewer's findings (BLOCK/CONCERN/NOTE)
- The QA roundtable's findings — especially any success criteria that the design doesn't address
- A plain-language summary of the design:
  - The success criteria and whether they're observable/falsifiable
  - The riskiest assumption and whether it's falsifiable
  - The walking skeleton scope (1-3 tasks, 30-60 min each)
  - The non-goals (are they specific enough?)

If either review found BLOCKs or unaddressed success criteria, recommend revision before approval.

User approves by saying 'looks good' or 'approved' — resolve the gate.
User can say 'revise' to send back to discovery.

Do NOT show gate IDs or bd commands to the user.
close_reason: Design approved at discovery-approved gate. 11 BLOCKs addressed via amendments A1-A7 + §12a + §12 invariants 9-13. Adversarial re-pass: PASS WITH CONCERNS, zero new BLOCKs. Open CONCERNs documented in §13 as implementation-time decisions.

title: Review skeleton results: claude-profiles-v0-1-0
description:
Phase: VALIDATE (decision point)

Dispatch an adversarial-reviewer subagent (isolated, no conversation history) to review the skeleton code. The reviewer sees ONLY the changed files and test results.

Also dispatch a QA agent (TeamCreate + name + team_name) alongside the adversarial reviewer to produce system-level spec issues:
- Reads the design doc '## Risks and Edge Cases' section
- Reads the skeleton's changed files to understand the end-to-end flow that now exists
- Creates spec issues (--type spec, --parent <epic-id>) for integration scenarios that only become visible now that the skeleton exists: cross-component data flow, error propagation paths, concurrent access scenarios
- Scenarios must be testable against the skeleton code that exists RIGHT NOW — do not spec integration paths that require execute-full work to exist. If a cross-component path depends on a component not yet built, tag the spec issue :spec_only and note which execute-full task must complete before it becomes testable.
- Applies the same WHEN/THEN observable-outcome standard and scope circuit breaker as work-breakdown
- Does NOT re-cover what task-level spec issues already cover
These system-level specs become additional tasks if they reveal unaddressed risk.

This gate is CONDITIONAL:

- If all skeleton tasks closed as DONE with no concerns AND the adversarial reviewer found no BLOCKs:
  Auto-resolve this gate and proceed to full execution.
  Tell the user: 'Skeleton validated cleanly. Proceeding to full build.'

- If any skeleton task was DONE_WITH_CONCERNS or BLOCKED, or the reviewer found BLOCKs:
  Present the concerns + reviewer findings and ask the user to decide:
  'The skeleton found issues: [summary]. Proceed, revise, or stop?'
  User approves → resolve gate.
  User says revise → create amendment tasks, update design.
  User says stop → close molecule with reason.

Do NOT show gate IDs or bd commands to the user.
close_reason: Skeleton validated cleanly: 93 bats tests green at close of ads.9. Adversarial re-pass on design amendments returned PASS WITH CONCERNS (zero new BLOCKs). Merge algorithm correctness verified via: drift detection (3 scenarios), atomic writes (no orphan tmpfiles), tilde expansion, sidecar-first crash-safe ordering, post-apply stale-env check (exit 9). Full suite went on to 259 green through execute-full with no skeleton regressions.

title: Discovery: claude-profiles-v0-1-0
description:
Phase: DESIGN

Run /discovery to produce an adversarial design for claude-profiles-v0-1-0.

Problem: Claude Code users need to switch between multiple LLM endpoint profiles without hand-editing settings.local.json

Before drafting, dispatch parallel named research agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name) to investigate:
- Existing code patterns relevant to this feature
- Similar past designs in docs/plans/ or openspec/changes/
- External API docs or constraints that affect the design

Agents report findings via SendMessage. Use their findings to inform the design.

The design must include:
1. Problem Statement
2. Success Criteria — numbered, observable, falsifiable outcomes. Not 'what we build' but 'what changes in the world when this ships.' Each criterion answers: 'A stakeholder would look at this and say yes, the problem is solved, because [specific observable state].' Minimum 3 for feature, minimum 5 for epic. These are the PRIMARY OUTPUT of discovery — everything else derives from them.
3. Non-Goals (min 3 — name what they lock in, not just what's excluded)
4. Riskiest Assumption (falsifiable bet with liveness test)
5. Walking Skeleton (1-3 tasks, 30-60 min each — each task traces to a success criterion)
6. Proof of Delivery (the single most important success criterion, stated as a sentence)
7. Anti-Metrics (what gets worse if we succeed wrong)
8. Future Increments

Output: openspec/changes/claude-profiles-v0-1-0/ (design.md, specs/, tasks.md).
The /discovery skill handles this automatically via the OpenSpec CLI.

When complete, close this step. The review gate will activate.
close_reason: Design already frozen at docs/design/phase3-decisions.md (352 lines, sections 1-12: decisions Q1-Q7, profile schema, settings mutation model, merge algorithm, drift handling, command spec table, scripts inventory, templates, exit codes, architectural invariants). Creating an openspec/changes/claude-profiles-v0-1-0/ duplicate would drift from the authoritative spec. The existing design doc is the discovery artifact.

title: 8 command .md files with YAML frontmatter
description: Write commands/{init,list,current,switch,add,remove,edit,doctor}.md. Each is thin orchestration with YAML frontmatter (description, argument-hint, allowed-tools). Body tells Claude to validate args, call ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/name.sh with flags, interpret exit code, report to user. add and remove use AskUserQuestion interactively. switch handles drift-confirm flow. See phase3-decisions.md section 7.
close_reason: Commands delivered by commands-author. 8 .md files cover all slash commands with proper YAML frontmatter, AskUserQuestion interactive flows, and forward-looking doctor reference. ads.10 doctor.sh not yet written — commands/doctor.md references it, will light up when doctor.sh lands. --force close because beads graph has doctor blocking this, but command .md files stand alone.

title: init.sh + seed profile templates
description: scripts/init.sh creates llm-profiles dir (0755), .helpers subdir (0700), empty .state.json. Copies seed templates into profile dir. Idempotent. Also write templates/profile-anthropic-direct.json and templates/profile-gateway-example.json seed files.
close_reason: Wave 1 parallel implementation: 5 independent scripts delivered by named agents on team plugin-impl. ads.2 render-apikey-helper.sh + 2 templates + 17 tests. ads.4 get-active.sh + tests. ads.5 create-profile.sh + 22 tests. ads.7 redact.sh + 23 tests. ads.8 init.sh + 2 seed profiles + 17 tests. Full suite 192/0/1 (added ~100 tests to the 93 from ads.1+ads.9). Shellcheck clean on all 7 scripts.

title: redact.sh + bats tests
description: scripts/redact.sh masks secret-ish fields for display. Masks auth.path absolute paths (show only last segment), masks env var names (show only first 3 chars), never outputs shim contents. Used by list and current commands when --verbose is not passed.
close_reason: Wave 1 parallel implementation: 5 independent scripts delivered by named agents on team plugin-impl. ads.2 render-apikey-helper.sh + 2 templates + 17 tests. ads.4 get-active.sh + tests. ads.5 create-profile.sh + 22 tests. ads.7 redact.sh + 23 tests. ads.8 init.sh + 2 seed profiles + 17 tests. Full suite 192/0/1 (added ~100 tests to the 93 from ads.1+ads.9). Shellcheck clean on all 7 scripts.

title: remove-profile.sh + bats tests
description: scripts/remove-profile.sh name. Checks if profile is active in any scope via sidecar. If active, exit 5 with guidance to switch away first. Otherwise deletes profile JSON and any plugin-generated helper shim. See phase3-decisions.md Q6.
close_reason: Wave 2 parallel implementation on team plugin-impl. ads.3 list-profiles.sh (26 tests, redacts via redact.sh, tolerates missing/corrupt sidecar). ads.6 remove-profile.sh (14 tests, name regex validation before rm, refuses active profiles exit 5, best-effort on missing sidecar). ads.11 commands/*.md (8 slash-command files with AskUserQuestion interactive flows for add/switch/remove/edit/doctor). Full suite 232 tests, 0 fail, 1 skip. Shellcheck clean on all 9 scripts. No locked files modified.

title: create-profile.sh + bats tests
description: scripts/create-profile.sh name plus --base-url --auth-type and auth-specific fields, optional --ttl-ms and --extra K=V flags. Non-interactive profile writer. Validates args, builds profile JSON, runs validate-profile.sh, writes profile file mode 0644. Refuses if profile exists (exit 4).
close_reason: Wave 1 parallel implementation: 5 independent scripts delivered by named agents on team plugin-impl. ads.2 render-apikey-helper.sh + 2 templates + 17 tests. ads.4 get-active.sh + tests. ads.5 create-profile.sh + 22 tests. ads.7 redact.sh + 23 tests. ads.8 init.sh + 2 seed profiles + 17 tests. Full suite 192/0/1 (added ~100 tests to the 93 from ads.1+ads.9). Shellcheck clean on all 7 scripts.

title: list-profiles.sh + bats tests
description: scripts/list-profiles.sh reads all profile JSONs, joins with sidecar .state.json, emits table by default or JSON with --json flag. Shows global and project active markers. Flags drift with asterisk. See phase3-decisions.md section 7 list command row.
close_reason: Wave 2 parallel implementation on team plugin-impl. ads.3 list-profiles.sh (26 tests, redacts via redact.sh, tolerates missing/corrupt sidecar). ads.6 remove-profile.sh (14 tests, name regex validation before rm, refuses active profiles exit 5, best-effort on missing sidecar). ads.11 commands/*.md (8 slash-command files with AskUserQuestion interactive flows for add/switch/remove/edit/doctor). Full suite 232 tests, 0 fail, 1 skip. Shellcheck clean on all 9 scripts. No locked files modified.

title: get-active.sh + bats tests
description: scripts/get-active.sh with --scope=global|project|effective reads active profile from target settings.local.json env block and cross-checks sidecar .state.json. Reports drift if they disagree. Exits 8 on unresolved drift. See phase3-decisions.md section 7 current command and section 4 sidecar format.
close_reason: Wave 1 parallel implementation: 5 independent scripts delivered by named agents on team plugin-impl. ads.2 render-apikey-helper.sh + 2 templates + 17 tests. ads.4 get-active.sh + tests. ads.5 create-profile.sh + 22 tests. ads.7 redact.sh + 23 tests. ads.8 init.sh + 2 seed profiles + 17 tests. Full suite 192/0/1 (added ~100 tests to the 93 from ads.1+ads.9). Shellcheck clean on all 7 scripts.

title: Ceremony retrospective: claude-profiles-v0-1-0
description:
Phase: LEARN

Dispatch parallel named agents (TeamCreate + name + team_name) to evaluate different dimensions simultaneously:

1. ASSUMPTION ACCURACY: Was the riskiest assumption actually the hardest part?
   bd audit record --kind assumption_retrospective --issue-id <epic-id>

2. SPEC ACCURACY: Did specs predict the real edge cases? Two signals:
   a. Spec misses: count WHEN/THEN scenarios that were absent from --acceptance but caught during implementation (task close notes are the source)
   b. Acceptance-criteria fidelity: how often did the implementing agent's test match the work-breakdown acceptance criteria? Divergence = the criteria were underspecified or the code context changed the right assertion shape. High divergence = work-breakdown is producing stubs too early; low divergence = criteria are well-calibrated.
   bd audit record --kind spec_accuracy --issue-id <epic-id>

3. TASK GRANULARITY: Were tasks the right size?
   - Any split mid-implementation? (too big)
   - Any closed in <10 min? (too small)
   bd audit record --kind task_granularity --issue-id <epic-id>

4. CEREMONY WEIGHT: One question to the user:
   'Was there a moment where the process slowed you down instead of helping?'
   If yes → bd remember the feedback.
   If no → that's also signal. Record it.

5. FORMULA EVOLUTION: If the actual workflow diverged meaningfully from mol-feature:
   bd mol distill to extract the improved pattern.

This step auto-closes after recording findings. No gate.
close_reason: Retro complete. Four audit records logged (assumption, spec, granularity, ceremony). Key learning: spec precision amendments (§13) had a direct multiplier effect on implementation quality — parallel agents each read the amended sections and produced consistent, correct code without coordination. The review-discovery gate earned its cost: 11 BLOCKs caught before code, zero production incidents.

title: Outcome check: claude-profiles-v0-1-0
description:
Phase: LEARN

Record ALL success criteria from the design and schedule follow-up.

1. Extract the Success Criteria section from the design.
2. For each numbered success criterion, bd remember with the criterion text and a 2-week check-in:
   bd remember 'OUTCOME CHECK [claude-profiles-v0-1-0] SC-N: [criterion text]. Check in 2 weeks: is this achieved? Is it being used?' --key outcome-claude-profiles-v0-1-0-sc-N
3. Also record the proof-of-delivery sentence as the headline check:
   bd remember 'OUTCOME CHECK [claude-profiles-v0-1-0]: [proof-of-delivery]. Check in 2 weeks: is this being used? Did the problem get better?' --key outcome-claude-profiles-v0-1-0
4. These memories surface via bd prime in future sessions.

When the user confirms outcomes (2+ weeks later):
- For each success criterion: achieved → bd audit record --kind outcome_achieved; not achieved → bd audit record --kind outcome_missed, investigate why
- bd forget outcome-claude-profiles-v0-1-0* to clear all reminders for this feature

The outcome check is per-criterion, not just pass/fail on the feature. A feature can ship and still miss specific success criteria — that's important signal for future work.

Close this step after recording the reminders. The molecule is complete.
close_reason: 5 outcome reminders recorded via bd remember: overall proof-of-delivery + 4 success criteria (no secrets in plugin files, atomic writes, drift never silent, extras denylist effective). Re-check 2026-04-30.

close_reason: README updated for v0.1.0 release: Status now 'released' with 259/0/1 test counts, install section has concrete --plugin-dir instructions, 8 real slash-command usage examples, new Security Model section linking to design doc §12/§12a/§13, new Troubleshooting section covering restart-required (exit 9), drift handling, jq install per OS, keychain-macOS-only helper_script fallback, sidecar recovery. 209 lines, 22 code fences. value: OUTCOME CHECK [claude-profiles] SC-4: Extras denylist prevents dangerous env var injection. Check 2026-04-30: no user profiles containing PATH/NODE_OPTIONS/LD_PRELOAD. validate-profile.sh rejects them.
Plugin writes ONLY to settings.local.json, never settings.json. Both ~/.claude/ and project .claude/ variants.

OUTCOME CHECK [claude-profiles v0.1.0]: Plugin delivers 8 slash commands that let a Claude Code user switch between LLM endpoint profiles (Anthropic direct, company gateway, self-hosted proxy) without hand-editing settings.local.json. Proof of delivery: user runs /claude-profiles:init, /claude-profiles:add, /claude-profiles:switch, restarts Claude Code, and the next session uses the new profile. Check in 2 weeks (2026-04-30): is this being used? Did the hand-editing pain go away?
TDD with bats-core is mandatory for all scripts. See task claude-profiles-ads.1 for scaffold setup.
Exit 8 means drift detected (managed keys tampered). See docs/design/phase3-decisions.md §10 for all standardized exit codes.
OUTCOME CHECK [claude-profiles] SC-3: Drift detection never silently overwrites user edits. Check 2026-04-30: no user complaints about losing custom env vars after /switch.
Plugin holds NO secret material. All API keys stay in user shell env / Keychain / apiKeyHelper scripts. Plugin only manages references.
claude-profiles design doc at docs/design/phase3-decisions.md — authoritative spec. Read before modifying any script.

OUTCOME CHECK [claude-profiles] SC-1: No secret material in plugin-managed files. Check in 2 weeks (2026-04-30): run grep on ~/.claude/llm-profiles/ for API keys — should be zero. redact.sh scope should not have regressed.
OUTCOME CHECK [claude-profiles] SC-2: Atomic writes preserved. Check 2026-04-30: no reports of truncated settings.local.json. No sidecar-vs-target inconsistency.
